title: SonicWall High-Volume Firewall Denies from Single Source (Reconnaissance)
spl:
index=netfw (sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" OR sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall") earliest=-1h
| eval act=lower(coalesce(action, fw_action, act))
| eval src_ip=coalesce(src_ip, src, c_src)
| eval dest_ip=coalesce(dest_ip, dst, c_dst)
| eval dest_port=coalesce(dest_port, dst_port, dpt, service)
| where match(act,"(?i)deny|drop|block|reject")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port
| where count > 75
| sort -count
| head 25
description: Concentrated denied flows from one source toward many destinations or ports typically indicate Internet-wide scanning, brute forcing, or a compromised internal host attempting lateral movement through the firewall.
value: Security analysts detect network reconnaissance by classifying high-volume SonicWall deny events into scan types (host scan, port scan, compound scan), filtering out authorized vulnerability scanners, and identifying both external attackers and potentially compromised internal hosts.
implementation: Normalise `action` fields using the TA’s CIM aliases. Adjust the deny count threshold for your enterprise’s NAT and scanner noise profile. Enrich top sources with ASN and threat intelligence lookups; auto-submit to SOAR for containment on critical assets.
visualization: Table (top sources), Bar chart (denies by destination port), Map (src_ip geolocation).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall
Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk

knownFalsePositives: Access denied for users in mid-onboarding, role changes pending propagation, or VDI sessions with new device IDs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• SonicWall syslog to Splunk. Data in `index=firewall` with `sourcetype=sonicwall`. Key fields: `msg_id` (264=Connection Dropped, 36=Connection Opened then Dropped, 173=Fragmented Packet Dropped), `src`, `srcPort`, `dst`, `dstPort`, `proto`, `fw_action` (drop/deny/block).
• Reconnaissance signatures: a single source IP sending connections to many destination IPs (host scan), many destination ports (port scan), or both (compound scan). The volume and pattern distinguish legitimate traffic (CDN, DNS) from malicious scanning.
• Create a `trusted_scanners.csv` lookup: `src_ip,scanner_name,owner,authorized` to whitelist known vulnerability scanners and monitoring tools.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify deny events:
```spl
index=firewall sourcetype=sonicwall fw_action IN ("drop","deny","block") earliest=-1h
| stats count
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Reconnaissance detection:
```spl
index=firewall sourcetype=sonicwall fw_action IN ("drop","deny","block") earliest=-1h
| stats count dc(dst) as unique_targets dc(dstPort) as unique_ports by src
| lookup trusted_scanners.csv src_ip as src OUTPUT authorized
| where isnull(authorized)
| eval scan_type=case(
    unique_targets > 20 AND unique_ports > 20, "Compound Scan",
    unique_targets > 20, "Host Scan",
    unique_ports > 20, "Port Scan",
    count > 500, "High-Volume Deny",
    1==1, "Low"
  )
| where scan_type!="Low"
| sort - count
| iplocation src
| table src, Country, count, unique_targets, unique_ports, scan_type
```

Understanding this SPL: Classifies high-volume deny sources into scan types. A host scan (many targets, few ports) targets a specific service across the network — often looking for exposed SSH, RDP, or SMB. A port scan (one target, many ports) enumerates services on a single host — precursor to targeted exploitation. A compound scan is the most aggressive. After excluding authorized scanners, all flagged IPs warrant investigation.

Port scan targeting analysis:
```spl
index=firewall sourcetype=sonicwall fw_action IN ("drop","deny","block") earliest=-1h
| stats count by src, dstPort
| eventstats sum(count) as total_from_src by src
| where total_from_src > 200
| sort src, - count
| head 50
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Run `nmap -sS [target]` from an external test host. Verify the deny events and scan classification.
(b) Verify trusted scanners are excluded.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Active scanners (last hour). Row 2 — Scan type breakdown. Row 3 — Top denied ports.
Alert: Immediate on Compound Scan. Informational on Host/Port Scan.

Runbook:
1. **External host scan** — Block source IP at the border. Report to abuse contact.
2. **Internal host scan** — Potential compromised host. Isolate and investigate.
3. **High-volume deny from known IP** — May be a misconfigured application. Check with app owner.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Too many false positives** — Raise thresholds. CDN/DNS IPs hit many destinations legitimately.
• **No deny events** — SonicWall may have an implicit allow rule. Check firewall rules.
grandmaExplanation: When someone tries to knock on a lot of doors that are locked, it usually means they are looking for one that is open. We count how many locked doors each visitor tries, and if the number is unusually high, we flag them as suspicious and investigate.
